name: Manufacturing Master ValuePack
description: Foundation intelligence pack for manufacturing sector covering discrete, process, advanced, contract, and supply chain operations. Provides signal-to-hypothesis reasoning, KPI benchmarking, persona profiling, and value quantification for manufacturing enterprise sales and advisory engagements.

taxonomies.description: Production of distinct, countable items through assembly, fabrication, or machining. Characterized by BOM complexity, routing optimization, and work-in-process tracking.
Production through formulation, blending, or chemical transformation. Characterized by recipe/formula management, batch consistency, yield optimization, and continuous flow operations.
Technology-intensive manufacturing leveraging automation, digital twins, AI/ML, and novel materials. High R&D intensity, rapid innovation cycles, and capital-intensive operations.
Third-party production for brand owners. Margin pressure, capacity utilization optimization, and customer compliance are defining characteristics.
Cross-functional capabilities spanning all manufacturing segments. Focus on flow efficiency, cost optimization, risk mitigation, and operational excellence.

pains.name: Unplanned Equipment Downtime
pains.description: Unexpected machine failures causing production line stoppages, emergency repairs, and schedule disruptions.
symptoms: maintenance costs exceeding 15% of operating budget
increasing frequency of emergency work orders

pains.name: Poor First Pass Yield / High Rework
pains.description: Products failing quality checks on initial production run, requiring rework, scrap, or concession processing.

pains.name: Inventory Overstock and Obsolescence
pains.description: Excess raw materials, WIP, and finished goods consuming working capital while creating obsolescence risk.

pains.name: Production Schedule Disruption
pains.description: Inability to meet planned production schedules due to material shortages, labor gaps, or equipment issues.

pains.name: Supplier Quality Failures and Delays
pains.description: Incoming materials or components from suppliers not meeting specifications or arriving late, disrupting production.

pains.name: Labor Shortage and Skill Gaps
pains.description: Inability to attract, retain, or upskill production workforce causing capacity constraints and knowledge loss.
symptoms: training spend increasing with flat output

pains.name: Energy Cost Escalation and Carbon Pressure
pains.description: Rising energy costs combined with Scope 1/2 emissions reporting requirements squeezing margins.

pains.name: Regulatory Compliance and Audit Burden
pains.description: Growing complexity of regulatory requirements (OSHA, EPA, FDA, ISO) consuming management attention and creating violation risk.

pains.name: Warranty and Field Failure Costs
pains.description: Post-sale product failures generating warranty claims, service costs, and brand damage.

pains.name: Poor Production Visibility and Data Silos
pains.description: Lack of real-time operational visibility due to disconnected systems, manual data collection, and legacy IT.

pains.name: Inefficient Changeover and Setup Times
pains.description: Long changeover periods between product runs reducing effective capacity and increasing costs.

pains.name: Capacity Constraint and Bottleneck Management
pains.description: Inability to identify, measure, and elevate production bottlenecks limiting overall throughput.
symptoms: One work center dictates overall output
queue times >40% of total lead time

pains.name: Forecast Inaccuracy and Demand Volatility
pains.description: Inability to predict demand leading to mismatches between supply and demand across the network.

pains.name: High Logistics and Transportation Costs
pains.description: Freight, warehousing, and distribution costs eroding margins due to poor network design or execution.

pains.name: Workforce Safety and Incident Frequency
pains.description: Workplace injuries, near-misses, and safety violations creating costs, liability, and operational disruption.

pains.name: Inadequate Preventive Maintenance Programs
pains.description: Reactive maintenance culture with insufficient planning leading to higher costs and shorter asset life.

pains.name: Long Production Lead Times
pains.description: Extended elapsed time from order release to completion reducing responsiveness and increasing WIP.
symptoms: increasing queue time at work centers
batch sizes larger than MOQ justify

pains.name: Product Traceability and Genealogy Gaps
pains.description: Inability to track component lot/batch history through production to finished goods for recall or compliance.

pains.name: Raw Material Cost Volatility and Sourcing Risk
pains.description: Commodity price swings and geopolitical supply disruptions threatening margin stability.
symptoms: force majeure events in past 24 months
no dual-source strategy for critical items

pains.name: Ineffective Production Planning and APS
pains.description: Legacy planning systems unable to handle multi-constraint optimization and real-time disruptions.

pains.name: Poor New Product Introduction (NPI) Ramp
pains.description: Slow or costly transition from prototype to volume production missing market windows.

pains.name: Contract Manufacturing Margin Compression
pains.description: CMO/EMS/CDMO facing price-down pressure from customers while input costs rise.
symptoms: quoted lead times longer than competitors

pains.name: MES/SCADA Legacy Modernization Debt
pains.description: Aging manufacturing execution systems creating integration barriers, security risks, and functional gaps.

pains.name: Quality Management System Inefficiency
pains.description: Manual or fragmented QMS processes slowing documentation, increasing errors, and complicating audits.

pains.name: Sustainability Reporting and Scope 3 Complexity
pains.description: Increasing pressure to measure, report, and reduce emissions across operations and supply chain.

kpis.name: Overall Equipment Effectiveness
kpis.formula: (Good Units × Ideal Cycle Time) / Planned Production Time OR Availability × Performance × Quality

kpis.name: Mean Time Between Failures
kpis.formula: Total Operating Time / Number of Failures

kpis.name: Mean Time To Repair
kpis.formula: Total Repair Time / Number of Repair Events

kpis.name: First Pass Yield
kpis.formula: (Units Passing First Inspection / Total Units Produced) × 100

kpis.name: Scrap Rate
kpis.formula: (Scrap Units / Total Units Started) × 100 OR (Scrap Cost / Total Material Cost) × 100

kpis.name: Cost of Poor Quality
kpis.formula: (Internal Failure Costs + External Failure Costs + Appraisal Costs + Prevention Costs) / Total Revenue × 100

kpis.name: Defects Per Million Opportunities
kpis.formula: (Number of Defects / Number of Defect Opportunities) × 1,000,000

kpis.name: WIP to Total Inventory Ratio
kpis.formula: WIP Inventory Value / Total Inventory Value × 100

kpis.name: Expediting Cost
kpis.formula: Total Cost of Rush Orders + Premium Freight + Overtime

kpis.name: Supplier Parts Per Million Defects
kpis.formula: (Defective Parts from Supplier / Total Parts Received) × 1,000,000

kpis.name: Supplier Risk Score
kpis.formula: Weighted composite of financial, operational, geopolitical, and single-source risk indicators

kpis.name: Labor Productivity
kpis.formula: Output Value (or Units) / Labor Hours or Labor Cost

kpis.name: Energy Intensity
kpis.formula: Total Energy Consumed / Output Units or Revenue

kpis.name: Carbon Intensity
kpis.formula: Total CO2e Emissions / Output Units or Revenue

kpis.name: Regulatory Audit Findings
kpis.formula: Count of findings per audit cycle

kpis.name: CAPA Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average Days from CAPA Open to Close

kpis.name: Production Data Latency
kpis.formula: Time from event occurrence to data availability in reporting system

kpis.name: Operational Decision Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average time from signal detection to management action

kpis.name: Changeover Time
kpis.formula: Average time to switch production from one product to another

valueDrivers.name: Downtime Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Reducing unplanned equipment downtime through predictive maintenance, reliability engineering, and faster repair processes.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Avoided lost production value + reduced emergency maintenance spend + deferred capital replacement

valueDrivers.name: Yield Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Improving first pass yield and reducing defect rates through process control, SPC, and quality analytics.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Reduced material waste + reduced rework labor + reduced inspection overhead

valueDrivers.name: Scrap and Rework Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Systematic reduction of scrap, rework, and concession processing through root cause analysis and process improvement.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Direct material recovery + labor productivity gain + reduced quality hold inventory

valueDrivers.name: Throughput Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Increasing effective production output without proportional capital investment through bottleneck management, OEE improvement, and cycle time reduction.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Incremental margin on additional units sold + deferred capacity expansion

valueDrivers.name: Labor Productivity Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Increasing output per labor hour through automation, skills development, workflow optimization, and reduced overtime.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Reduced labor cost per unit + reduced overtime premium + reduced hiring/training costs

valueDrivers.name: Working Capital Improvement
valueDrivers.description: Reducing cash tied up in inventory, receivables, and work-in-process through better planning, scheduling, and supplier management.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Freed cash at WACC + reduced obsolescence write-offs + reduced carrying cost

valueDrivers.name: Direct Cost Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Broad category of operational cost reduction including energy, logistics, maintenance, and overhead efficiency.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Reduced COGS + reduced SG&A operations component

valueDrivers.name: Revenue Uplift
valueDrivers.description: Top-line growth enabled by improved on-time delivery, new capacity, faster NPI, and better customer service.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Incremental revenue from higher fill rates + new customer acquisition + price premium for reliability

valueDrivers.name: Risk and Compliance Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Reducing regulatory, safety, supply chain, and quality risks that could result in fines, recalls, or business interruption.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Avoided fines + avoided recall costs + reduced insurance premiums + avoided customer penalties

valueDrivers.name: Warranty Cost Reduction
valueDrivers.description: Reducing post-sale product failures and warranty claims through design improvement, process control, and field analytics.
valueDrivers.valueMechanism: Direct warranty spend reduction + reduced service parts inventory + brand protection

formulas.name: Downtime Cost Avoidance
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Downtime Cost Avoidance = (Baseline Downtime Hours - Target Downtime Hours) × Hourly Production Value
requiredInputs: Hourly production value ($/hour = Revenue / Scheduled Hours)
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 500 hours → 200 hours at $15K/hour = $4.5M annual savings

formulas.name: OEE Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Target OEE - Baseline OEE) × Scheduled Production Hours × Hourly Production Value × Margin Rate
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 60% → 75% OEE, 8,000 hrs, $12K/hr, 25% margin = $3.6M annual contribution

formulas.name: Scrap Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Savings = (Baseline Scrap Rate - Target Scrap Rate) × Annual Material Spend × (1 - Recovery Value %)

formulas.name: First Pass Yield Financial Impact
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Target FPY - Baseline FPY) × Annual Units × (Material Cost per Unit + Rework Labor per Unit + Inspection Cost per Unit)

confidenceRules.condition: Cost data from BOM and labor standards

formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 85% → 95% FPY, 1M units, $50 material + $8 rework + $2 inspect = $600K savings

formulas.name: Inventory Reduction Cash Release
formulas.formulaExpression: Cash Released = (Baseline Inventory Days - Target Inventory Days) × (COGS / 365) × Working Capital Cost of Carry %
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 60 → 35 days, $200M COGS, 12% WACC = $1.64M cash released

formulas.name: Inventory Turn Improvement Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Target Turns - Baseline Turns) × Average Inventory Value × Cost of Carry % + Obsolescence Reduction

formulas.name: Warranty Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Savings = Baseline Warranty Cost × (Reduction Target %) + Avoided Recall Risk Value
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 2% → 1% of $500M revenue, 5% recall prob, $10M/event = $5.5M expected value

formulas.name: Throughput Revenue Uplift
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Revenue Uplift = (Target Throughput - Baseline Throughput) × Selling Price per Unit × Market Absorption %

formulas.name: Labor Productivity Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Savings = (Target Productivity - Baseline Productivity) / Target Productivity × Total Labor Cost + Overtime Reduction

formulas.name: Changeover Time Reduction Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Baseline Changeover Hours - Target Changeover Hours) × Hourly Production Value × Margin Rate

formulas.name: Energy Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Savings = (Baseline Energy Intensity - Target Energy Intensity) × Annual Output × Energy Price per Unit

formulas.name: Freight Cost Optimization
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Savings = (Baseline Cost per Unit Shipped - Target Cost per Unit) × Annual Units Shipped + Mode Optimization Savings

confidenceRules.condition: Freight cost data from 3PL or TMS

formulas.name: Safety Incident Cost Reduction
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Baseline TRIR - Target TRIR) × Total Hours / 200,000 × (Direct Cost per Incident + Indirect Cost Multiplier) + Insurance Premium Reduction
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 4.0 → 2.0 TRIR, 1M hrs, $15K direct, 5x indirect, $2M premium = $3.25M + $0.4M

formulas.name: Supplier Quality Failure Cost
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Cost Avoided = (Baseline Supplier PPM - Target Supplier PPM) / 1,000,000 × Annual Receipt Volume × (Inspection Cost + Production Disruption Cost + Scrap Cost)
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 2000 → 500 PPM, 10M receipts, $500 inspect + $2K disrupt + $50 scrap = $17.5M

formulas.name: CAPA Cycle Time Efficiency
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Baseline CAPA Cycle Days - Target Days) × Average Open CAPAs × Cost per Day of Delay + Audit Readiness Value
requiredInputs: Cost per CAPA day (labor + hold inventory + delayed release)

formulas.name: Predictive Maintenance ROI
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Avoided Downtime Cost + (Preventive Cost - Predictive Cost) + Extended Asset Life Value
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: $3M → $0.5M downtime, $1M PM → $0.8M predictive, $10M asset + 2 yrs = $2.7M + $0.2M + $0.5M = $3.4M

formulas.name: Demand Forecast Accuracy Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Baseline MAPE - Target MAPE) / 100 × Annual Revenue × Inventory Cost % + Stockout Revenue Recovery

formulas.name: MES Integration Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Labor Productivity Gain + Quality Cost Reduction + Paperwork Elimination + Decision Latency Reduction
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: 5 FTE paperwork, $75/hr, 2 hrs latency, 3% throughput = $0.78M + $0.5M + $0.3M = $1.58M

formulas.name: NPI Acceleration Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Baseline Time to Market - Target Time to Market) × Monthly Revenue per Product × Product Count + Ramp Yield Improvement Value

formulas.name: Compliance Automation Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Audit Preparation Labor Reduction + Finding Remediation Cost Reduction + Avoided Penalty Risk Value

formulas.name: Capacity Utilization Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = (Target Utilization - Baseline Utilization) × Total Capacity Value × Incremental Margin %

formulas.name: Carbon Reduction Financial Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Energy Savings + Carbon Credit/Offset Value + Tax Incentive Value + Avoided Carbon Tax/Compliance Cost
requiredInputs: Carbon tax or ETS cost per ton

formulas.name: CMO Margin Expansion
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Revenue × (Target Gross Margin - Baseline Gross Margin) + Capacity Utilization Gain Value

formulas.name: Warehouse Efficiency Improvement
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Labor Cost Reduction + Space Optimization Value + Pick Accuracy Improvement Value

formulas.name: S&OP Process Maturity Value
formulas.formulaExpression: Annual Value = Forecast Accuracy Improvement Value + Inventory Reduction Value + Expediting Cost Reduction + Cross-Functional Efficiency Gain
formulas.exampleCalculation: Example: Level 2 → Level 4, 10% forecast, 15% inventory, $1M → $0.2M expedite = $5M + $3M + $0.8M

benchmarks.name: COPQ as % of Revenue - World Class
Freight as % of Revenue - Best Practice

signalRules.signalName: Job Posting Surge for Maintenance Roles
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Multiple maintenance technician and reliability engineer postings in <90 days
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Signal of aging asset base and/or reactive maintenance crisis; likely unplanned downtime pain
requiredConfirmationSignals: Asset age proxy from satellite imagery

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 10-K mentions supply chain disruption as material risk for 2+ consecutive years
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Board-level recognition of supply chain vulnerability; procurement transformation or dual-sourcing initiatives likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Management cites margin pressure from input costs or operational inefficiency in 2+ consecutive quarters
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Operational cost reduction programs likely being initiated; efficiency technology interest elevated

signalRules.signalName: FDA Warning Letter or Consent Decree
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public FDA enforcement action citing manufacturing quality or data integrity
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Critical quality system failure; QMS modernization, CAPA remediation, and validation services demand imminent

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: OSHA public enforcement data shows serious violation in past 12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: EHS management system gaps; safety culture and compliance technology investment trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Sustainability rating downgrade (CDP, MSCI, Sustainalytics) in past 12 months
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Reputational and customer retention risk; Scope 1/2/3 measurement and reduction programs likely prioritized

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 10-K or earnings show warranty reserve growth disproportionate to revenue
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Field quality crisis or new product quality ramp issues; root cause analytics and quality system investment trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Balance sheet shows inventory growing faster than revenue for 2+ quarters
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Working capital pressure; demand planning, inventory optimization, or supplier reliability issues probable

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Capex guidance reduced significantly while asset base ages
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Deferred modernization creating reliability and efficiency debt; equipment failure risk rising

signalRules.signalName: New CIO Appointment from Outside Industry
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: External CIO hire with digital transformation background
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Legacy system modernization likely priority; MES/ERP/SCADA integration and data architecture investments probable

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New CEO or COO from operational excellence background (e.g., former Toyota, GE, Danaher)
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Operational transformation program likely within 12-18 months; Lean/Six Sigma/TPM tool demand elevated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: 10-K shows single customer exceeding 30% of revenue
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Customer dependency risk; capacity planning and contract manufacturing dynamics acute

signalRules.signalName: R&D Spending Increase with Patent Decline
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: R&D spend up but patent filings or product launches flat/down
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: NPI inefficiency or technology transfer gaps; engineering-to-manufacturing bridge likely needed

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public labor dispute or contract negotiation at manufacturing facilities
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Labor cost and workforce planning pressure; automation and workforce scheduling investment trigger

signalRules.signalName: Energy Price Spike in Key Regions
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Natural gas or electricity prices spike >30% in facility regions
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Energy cost crisis; efficiency retrofit, renewable procurement, and process optimization demand elevated

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Key supplier files Chapter 11 or declares force majeure
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Immediate sourcing crisis; supplier diversification and supply chain risk management investment trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public recall or market withdrawal of manufactured product
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Severe quality system failure; traceability, QMS, and root cause analytics demand acute

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Issuance of sustainability-linked financing instrument
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Measurable ESG commitments with financial penalties; emissions tracking and reduction technology demand

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Public announcement of new ERP selection or implementation
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: System integration opportunity window; MES, WMS, QMS, and planning system integration demand during project

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: PE firm acquires manufacturing company with operational improvement thesis
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Aggressive EBITDA improvement targets within 3-5 years; operational excellence and cost reduction tool demand high

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: SEC 8-K or public disclosure of cybersecurity incident affecting operations
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: OT/IT security gap; ICS security assessment and segmentation investment trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New tariffs, sanctions, or trade restrictions affecting imported materials or exports
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Sourcing restructuring; nearshoring, supplier diversification, and trade compliance investment trigger

signalRules.signalName: Board Addition of Supply Chain Expert
signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New independent director with supply chain or operations expertise
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Board-level focus on supply chain resilience; strategic sourcing and risk management investment likely

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Gross margin declining for 3+ consecutive quarters
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Cost structure crisis; operational efficiency, yield improvement, and cost reduction programs imminent

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: New facility, line, or capacity expansion announced
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Scale-up risk; planning, training, quality system, and commissioning services demand

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Order backlog growing significantly with flat or declining output
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Capacity constraint or throughput bottleneck; bottleneck elevation and scheduling optimization trigger

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Multiple job postings for IIoT, digital twin, or predictive analytics roles
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Industry 4.0/digital transformation initiative underway; data infrastructure and analytics platform demand

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Loss or suspension of ISO 9001, AS9100, IATF 16949, or similar certification
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Quality management system critical failure; QMS overhaul and re-certification support demand

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Trend toward outsourcing production to CMOs/EMS/CDMOs
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Internal manufacturing footprint rationalization; CMO management and quality oversight technology demand

signalRules.rawSignalPattern: Customer NPS or satisfaction scores declining over 2+ measurement periods
signalRules.interpretedMeaning: Customer experience crisis potentially rooted in quality or delivery failures; root cause and improvement trigger

evidenceSources.name: SEC 10-K Annual Report
evidenceSources.description: Risk factors, financial metrics, warranty reserves, inventory levels, revenue by segment

evidenceSources.name: SEC 10-Q Quarterly Report
evidenceSources.description: Quarterly financial trends, margin changes, inventory growth, capex patterns

evidenceSources.name: Earnings Call Transcripts
evidenceSources.description: Management commentary on operations, margins, guidance, strategic priorities

evidenceSources.name: Job Posting Data (LinkedIn, Indeed)
evidenceSources.description: Hiring patterns revealing technology investments, expansion plans, skill gaps

evidenceSources.name: FDA Enforcement Database
evidenceSources.description: Warning letters, 483 observations, import alerts, consent decrees

evidenceSources.name: OSHA Enforcement Data
evidenceSources.description: Inspection results, citations, penalties by establishment

evidenceSources.name: Industry Association Reports
evidenceSources.description: NAM, AEM, IPC, FMI sector surveys and outlooks

evidenceSources.name: Consulting Firm Research
evidenceSources.description: McKinsey, BCG, Deloitte, PwC operations and supply chain studies

evidenceSources.name: Credit Rating Agency Reports
evidenceSources.description: Moody's, S&P, Fitch credit analysis with operational risk commentary

evidenceSources.name: Trade and Industry Publications
evidenceSources.description: IndustryWeek, Plant Engineering, Quality Magazine, Food Engineering

evidenceSources.name: Patent and Trademark Filings
evidenceSources.description: USPTO filings revealing R&D direction and technology investments

buyingTriggers.name: Major Unplanned Downtime Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Catastrophic equipment failure causing >24hr production loss
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Emergency procurement possible; expedited vendor selection; budget from operations contingency or insurance

buyingTriggers.name: New ERP/MES Selection
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Organization initiating ERP or MES replacement project
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Large capital project; integration vendors and planning modules bundled; steering committee decision

buyingTriggers.name: New Plant or Line Commissioning
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Greenfield or brownfield expansion with new equipment and systems
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Capital project budget; design-influence window; long-term vendor relationship opportunity

buyingTriggers.name: Regulatory Warning Letter
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: FDA, OSHA, or equivalent regulatory enforcement action
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Compliance-driven; urgency high; legal/compliance leads; budget from legal reserve or quality

buyingTriggers.name: Private Equity Acquisition
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: PE buyout with operational improvement thesis
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: PE operating partner drives; 100-day plan; quick wins prioritized; EBITDA-focused vendor evaluation

buyingTriggers.name: CEO/COO Change
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New operations leader from transformation background
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: New strategy development; consulting engagement common; vendor evaluation reset

buyingTriggers.name: IPO Preparation
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Company preparing for public offering
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Systems and controls upgrade; audit readiness; scalable process documentation required

buyingTriggers.name: Customer Audit Failure
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major customer audit findings threatening contract
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Customer retention imperative; QMS/traceability investment; direct customer may influence vendor

buyingTriggers.name: Product Recall
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Public recall or market withdrawal
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Crisis response; traceability and root cause immediate; brand protection budget

buyingTriggers.name: Margin Compression Crisis
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Quarterly results miss due to operational cost overruns
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Cost reduction mandate; quick ROI solutions prioritized; CFO approval required

buyingTriggers.name: Cybersecurity Incident
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Ransomware or OT security breach affecting production
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Security-first evaluation; CISO/OT security lead; compliance and insurance drivers

buyingTriggers.name: Union Negotiation / Strike
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Labor dispute creating operational uncertainty
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Automation interest spikes; labor scheduling optimization; union consultation may be required

buyingTriggers.name: Supply Chain Disruption Event
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Force majeure, supplier bankruptcy, or geopolitical cutoff
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Supply chain transformation; dual-sourcing; supplier risk technology; procurement leads

buyingTriggers.name: Sustainability Linked Financing
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: SLB or SLL issuance creating measurable commitments
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Measurable ESG KPIs required; CSO/CFO joint sponsorship; penalty avoidance focus

buyingTriggers.name: Customer Sustainability Scorecard
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major customer imposes ESG/supplier sustainability requirements
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Customer retention driver; supply chain ESG data collection; may cascade to tier-2 suppliers

buyingTriggers.name: Aging Workforce Retirement Wave
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: >20% of skilled workforce eligible for retirement within 3 years
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Knowledge capture and automation; training technology; demographic-driven planning

buyingTriggers.name: Capacity Constraint - Backlog Crisis
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Order backlog exceeding 3 months or unable to accept new orders
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Revenue at risk; bottleneck analysis; capacity expansion vs efficiency decision

buyingTriggers.name: M&A Integration
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Post-merger operational integration of manufacturing footprints
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Synergy target pressure; duplicate system consolidation; best practice transfer

buyingTriggers.name: New Product Launch Delay
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: NPI timeline miss threatening market window
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Time-to-market recovery; DFM investment; ramp planning; engineering-manufacturing bridge

buyingTriggers.name: Digital Transformation Mandate
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Board or corporate mandate for Industry 4.0 / digital operations
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Strategic program; may have dedicated PMO; consulting engagement common; pilot-to-scale approach

buyingTriggers.name: Tariff / Trade Policy Change
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: New trade restrictions affecting material sourcing or export markets
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Sourcing restructuring; nearshoring evaluation; trade compliance technology; supply chain redesign

buyingTriggers.name: Energy Cost Spike
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Energy prices increase >30% in facility regions
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Utility budget crisis; energy efficiency audit; renewable procurement; process heat optimization

buyingTriggers.name: Competitive Factory Opening
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Competitor opens new highly automated facility threatening cost position
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Competitive response; benchmarking visit; automation business case; board visibility

buyingTriggers.name: ISO/AS Certification Loss
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Loss of quality or aerospace certification threatening customer eligibility
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Customer eligibility at risk; immediate QMS overhaul; re-certification project; may affect revenue

buyingTriggers.name: Customer Price-Down Mandate
buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major customer demands annual price reductions threatening viability
buyingTriggers.procurementImplications: Cost structure crisis; efficiency program; automation justification; make-vs-buy re-evaluation

personas.name: Chief Operating Officer
dislikedClaims: Vendor ROI claims without peer proof

personas.name: Plant Manager / GM
dislikedClaims: Metrics that don't reflect plant conditions

personas.name: VP Human Resources
dislikedClaims: Labor cost reduction without productivity gain

personas.name: Chief Procurement Officer
dislikedClaims: AI hype without spend data integration

personas.name: Operations Excellence Lead
personas.role: OpEx Director / Lean Six Sigma MBB

discoveryQuestions.name: OEE Measurement
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you currently measure and report OEE, and what is your plant-by-plant variation?

discoveryQuestions.name: Unplanned Downtime Root Cause
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your unplanned downtime events have identified root causes, and how long does root cause analysis typically take?

discoveryQuestions.name: Quality Cost Visibility
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Can you quantify your Cost of Poor Quality as a percentage of revenue, and how is it distributed across internal failure, external failure, appraisal, and prevention?

discoveryQuestions.name: Inventory Segmentation
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you segment inventory (raw, WIP, finished) and what percentage of your inventory is slow-moving or obsolete?

discoveryQuestions.name: Schedule Attainment Drivers
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What are the top three reasons your production schedule is not attained, and how do you measure each?

discoveryQuestions.name: Supplier Risk Exposure
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How many of your direct materials are single-sourced, and what is your process for identifying and qualifying alternative suppliers?

discoveryQuestions.name: Labor Turnover and Skills
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your annual turnover rate for skilled trades versus production operators, and how long does it take to fully certify a new hire?

discoveryQuestions.name: Energy and Carbon Baseline
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Do you have a validated baseline for Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions, and what is your largest source of energy consumption by facility?

discoveryQuestions.name: Compliance Audit Burden
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How many regulatory audits do you face annually, and what is the average preparation time and finding remediation cycle?

discoveryQuestions.name: Warranty Field Data
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you track and analyze field failure data, and what is the typical time from failure report to root cause identification?

discoveryQuestions.name: System Integration Landscape
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How many disconnected systems does your plant floor use for production data, and what is the latency from event to management report?

discoveryQuestions.name: Changeover Measurement
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Have you implemented SMED methodology, and what is your average changeover time by product family?

discoveryQuestions.name: Bottleneck Identification
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Do you know your current system constraint, and how much does it limit your overall throughput versus theoretical capacity?

discoveryQuestions.name: Forecast Process Maturity
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What is your current forecast accuracy (MAPE) at the SKU level, and how does your S&OP process handle demand volatility?

discoveryQuestions.name: Logistics Cost Structure
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you allocate freight costs (inbound vs outbound vs interplant), and what is your modal mix?

discoveryQuestions.name: Maintenance Strategy
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your maintenance is reactive vs preventive vs predictive, and what is your spare parts inventory strategy?

discoveryQuestions.name: Lead Time Composition
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How is your manufacturing lead time distributed across queue, setup, run, wait, and move time?

discoveryQuestions.name: Traceability Coverage
discoveryQuestions.questionText: What percentage of your production volume has full electronic traceability from raw material lot to customer shipment?

discoveryQuestions.name: CMO Margin Pressure
discoveryQuestions.questionText: How do you balance annual customer price-downs with input cost inflation, and what is your target gross margin trajectory?

discoveryQuestions.name: Digital Transformation Readiness
discoveryQuestions.questionText: Where are you on the Industry 4.0 maturity curve, and what are your top barriers to real-time production visibility?

objections.name: We already have a system
objections.description: Prospect claims existing systems address the need
objections.responseStrategy: Acknowledge investment; highlight integration gaps, data silos, or functional limitations; offer complementary value assessment

objections.name: No budget this year
objections.description: Budget cycle closed or capital constraints
objections.responseStrategy: Reframe as operational expense reduction or working capital release with self-funding model; offer pilot with minimal upfront

objections.name: Show me the ROI
objections.description: Demand for proven financial return before commitment
objections.responseStrategy: Provide peer benchmark case study with similar revenue/segment; offer paid proof-of-concept with predefined ROI measurement

objections.name: We can build this internally
objections.description: IT or engineering team prefers internal development
objections.responseStrategy: Compare build vs buy TCO over 5 years including maintenance, upgrades, and opportunity cost; highlight time-to-value gap

objections.name: Too disruptive to operations
objections.description: Concern about implementation impact on production
objections.responseStrategy: Propose phased rollout starting with non-critical line; offer parallel running; reference implementation at similar facility

objections.name: We need to focus on other priorities
objections.description: Competing initiatives consuming attention and resources
objections.responseStrategy: Connect to their stated strategic priorities in earnings or press releases; show how solution accelerates other initiatives

objections.name: Your solution is too expensive
objections.description: Price exceeds allocated budget or perceived value
objections.responseStrategy: Reframe total cost of inaction (downtime, scrap, working capital); offer modular pricing starting with highest-ROI module

objections.name: We need to see it work in our environment
objections.description: Skepticism about solution performance in their specific context
objections.responseStrategy: Offer site-specific pilot on one line or process with success criteria; provide customer reference visit to similar facility

objections.name: Our data is not ready
objections.description: Concerns about data quality, availability, or standardization
objections.responseStrategy: Offer data readiness assessment as first step; describe data cleansing and enrichment services; show progression path

objections.name: We tried this before and it failed
objections.description: Previous similar initiative did not deliver
objections.responseStrategy: Acknowledge failure; diagnose root cause (often change management, not technology); differentiate approach with governance and adoption focus

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
technologySystems.description: Core financial, procurement, and operational transaction system

technologySystems.name: Manufacturing Execution System (MES)
technologySystems.description: Shop-floor execution, tracking, and control system bridging ERP and equipment

technologySystems.name: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA)
technologySystems.description: Real-time monitoring and control of industrial processes and equipment

technologySystems.name: Distributed Control System (DCS)
technologySystems.description: Centralized control for continuous and batch process operations

technologySystems.name: Advanced Planning and Scheduling (APS)
technologySystems.description: Finite capacity planning and optimization across constraints

technologySystems.name: Warehouse Management System (WMS)
technologySystems.description: Inventory tracking, picking optimization, and warehouse operations

technologySystems.name: Transportation Management System (TMS)
technologySystems.description: Freight procurement, routing, and carrier management

technologySystems.name: Quality Management System (QMS)
technologySystems.description: Document control, CAPA, audit management, and compliance tracking

technologySystems.name: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS)
technologySystems.description: Sample tracking, test management, and lab workflow automation

technologySystems.name: Enterprise Asset Management (EAM/CMMS)
technologySystems.description: Asset registry, work order management, and maintenance planning

technologySystems.name: Condition Monitoring / Predictive Maintenance
technologySystems.description: Vibration, thermal, and oil analysis for predictive maintenance

technologySystems.name: Industrial IoT Platform
technologySystems.description: Edge-to-cloud data collection, analytics, and device management

technologySystems.name: Product Lifecycle Management (PLM)
technologySystems.description: BOM management, change control, and design collaboration

technologySystems.name: Supply Chain Planning (SCP/S&OP)
technologySystems.description: Demand planning, supply planning, and S&OP collaboration platform

technologySystems.name: Additive Manufacturing / 3D Printing
technologySystems.description: Direct digital manufacturing for prototyping and production

technologySystems.name: Digital Twin / Simulation
technologySystems.description: Virtual representation of physical assets for simulation and optimization

technologySystems.name: Robotic Process Automation / Cobots
technologySystems.description: Collaborative and industrial robots for assembly, handling, and inspection

technologySystems.name: Vision / AI Quality Inspection
technologySystems.description: Automated visual inspection using machine learning and computer vision

technologySystems.name: Environmental Health & Safety (EHS) Platform
technologySystems.description: Incident management, audit tracking, and regulatory compliance

technologySystems.name: Batch/Recipe Management
technologySystems.description: Formula management, batch execution, and electronic batch records

regulatoryFactors.name: OSHA General Industry Standards
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $15,625 per violation (serious), $156,259 per willful/repeat

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA Clean Air Act (Title V)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $37,500+ per day per violation

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA Clean Water Act (NPDES)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $66,086+ per violation per day

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA Current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Warning letter → consent decree → product seizure; potentially $100M+

regulatoryFactors.name: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $1,000-$500,000 per violation; mandatory recall authority

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $76,764 per violation per day

regulatoryFactors.name: ISO 9001 Quality Management
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of certification → customer exclusion; revenue risk

regulatoryFactors.name: IATF 16949 Automotive Quality
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of certification → OEM supplier exclusion

regulatoryFactors.name: AS9100 Aerospace Quality
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Loss of certification → defense/aerospace contract ineligibility

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA Greenhouse Gas Reporting
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $37,500+ per violation

regulatoryFactors.name: SEC Climate Disclosure Rules
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Enforcement actions; shareholder litigation

regulatoryFactors.name: OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM)
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $156,259 per willful violation; criminal referral possible

regulatoryFactors.name: EU REACH Regulation
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Market exclusion; fines per member state

regulatoryFactors.name: Conflict Minerals Rule (Dodd-Frank 1502)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Public companies using tin, tungsten, tantalum, gold
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: SEC enforcement; customer pressure

regulatoryFactors.name: ISO 14001 Environmental Management
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Customer audit findings; certification loss

regulatoryFactors.name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: No direct penalty; CISA enforcement for critical sectors

regulatoryFactors.name: EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Importers of steel, cement, aluminum, fertilizers, electricity, hydrogen into EU
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Border tariff on carbon intensity differential

regulatoryFactors.name: California Proposition 65
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Products sold in California with listed chemicals
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: $2,500+ per violation per day; bounty hunter lawsuits

regulatoryFactors.name: EPA TSCA Section 5/6
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: Civil penalties up to $50,000 per day; consent orders

regulatoryFactors.name: Medical Device Regulation (EU MDR)
regulatoryFactors.applicability: Medical device manufacturers selling in EU
regulatoryFactors.penaltyForNonCompliance: CE marking loss; market exclusion

competitorFactors.description: Competitors with deep vertical specialization (e.g., pharma MES, food traceability) command premium pricing and shorter sales cycles in their domains.
competitorFactors.impact: Differentiation vs horizontal platforms; requires segment-specific proof points

competitorFactors.description: Major ERP and MES vendors (SAP, Siemens, Dassault) leverage existing relationships to bundle adjacent solutions, creating competitive barriers.
competitorFactors.impact: Best-of-breed vendors must prove superior ROI and faster implementation; integration ease is critical

competitorFactors.description: Local SIs with long-standing plant relationships influence vendor selection through trust and implementation capability.
competitorFactors.impact: Channel and partnership strategy critical; direct sales alone insufficient in manufacturing

competitorFactors.description: Open source MES (e.g., OpenMES), low-code platforms, and commodity IoT devices pressure pricing for basic connectivity and visualization.
competitorFactors.impact: Must differentiate on analytics depth, regulatory compliance, and enterprise integration

competitorFactors.description: AWS, Azure, and GCP building manufacturing-specific vertical solutions (e.g., Azure Digital Twins, AWS IoT TwinMaker).
competitorFactors.impact: Infrastructure layer competition; value must be at application/analytics layer above cloud

competitorFactors.description: Private equity consolidating manufacturing software creating full-suite platforms through acquisition.
competitorFactors.impact: Point solutions face bundling pressure; best-of-breed must demonstrate superior depth

competitorFactors.description: Large manufacturers (e.g., Tesla, Siemens) developing proprietary solutions internally and potentially commercializing.
competitorFactors.impact: Mid-market vendors may find opening; enterprise sales require co-innovation partnerships

valueDomains.name: Downtime Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing unplanned and planned equipment downtime to increase available production time and asset utilization.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 15-25% reduction in unplanned downtime; 5-15% OEE improvement

valueDomains.name: Yield Improvement
valueDomains.description: Improving first-pass quality and reducing defect rates to minimize material waste and rework.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 2-5 percentage point FPY improvement; 10-30% COPQ reduction

valueDomains.name: Scrap/Rework Reduction
valueDomains.description: Systematic reduction of scrap, rework, and concession processing through root cause elimination.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 20-40% reduction in scrap and rework costs

valueDomains.name: Throughput Improvement
valueDomains.description: Increasing effective output without proportional capital investment through bottleneck elevation and cycle time reduction.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 10-20% throughput increase; deferred capacity expansion worth $5M-$50M

valueDomains.name: Labor Productivity
valueDomains.description: Increasing output per labor hour and reducing overtime through skills, automation, and workflow optimization.

valueDomains.name: Inventory Turns / Working Capital
valueDomains.description: Reducing cash tied in inventory while maintaining service levels through better planning and supplier management.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 20-40% inventory reduction; 2-4x turn improvement; $5M-$100M cash release

valueDomains.name: Energy Efficiency
valueDomains.description: Reducing energy consumption per unit of output through process optimization, equipment upgrade, and behavioral change.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 10-30% energy cost reduction; 15-25% carbon intensity improvement

valueDomains.name: Supplier Risk Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing supply disruption probability and impact through diversification, monitoring, and collaboration.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 50-70% reduction in supply disruption events; 10-20% supplier quality improvement

valueDomains.name: Warranty Cost Reduction
valueDomains.description: Reducing post-sale product failures and warranty claims through design and process improvement.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 20-40% warranty cost reduction; field failure rate halved

valueDomains.name: Compliance and Audit Readiness
valueDomains.description: Reducing compliance cost and audit risk through automated documentation, traceability, and quality system integration.
valueDomains.typicalFinancialImpact: 40-60% audit prep time reduction; 30-50% CAPA cycle time improvement; avoided penalties
